chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD. Cr. Bail Appln. No.S — 730 of 2022. DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE 13.07.2022. FOR ORDERS ON M.A. 6224/2022. FOR ORDERS ON OFFICE OBJECTION. FOR ORDERS ON M.A. 6225/2022. FOR HEARING OF MAIN CASE. Mr. Altaf Shahid Abro Advocate along with the applicant. ------ 1. Urgency granted. 2. Deferred. 3. Exemption allowed subject to all just exceptions. 4. The Applicants/accused Muhammad Ishaque and Hamzo seek protective bail in crime No.04 of 2022, registered at Police Station Khemi Jo Par District Tharparkar, under section 353, 188 PPC. It is inter-alia contended that applicants/accused are prepared to surrender before the learned trial court, but they apprehend their imminent arrest before reaching the said Court, as such, they seek protective bail to enable them to appear before the learned trial court for seeking pre-arrest bail. Serious malafides on the part of the complainant and the police have been alleged. Without touching merits of the case, in order to enable the Applicants to appear and surrender before the trial Court, the Applicants are admitted to protective bail for fifteen days from today subject to furnishing solvent surety in the sum of Rs.15000/-(Fifteen thousand) each and P.R. bond in the like sum to the satisfaction of Additional Registrar of this Court. The applicants / accused are directed to join the investigation. In case the Applicants/ accused fail to surrender before the trial Court within the time specified, surety furnished before this Court shall stand forfeited. This protective bail shall remain effective up to 27.07.2022 or when the applicants / accused surrender before the trial Court whichever is the earlier.

In case the Applicants/ accused fail to surrender before the trial Court within the time specified, surety furnished before this Court shall stand forfeited. This protective bail shall remain effective up to 27.07.2022 or when the applicants / accused surrender before the trial Court whichever is the earlier. This criminal bail application stands disposed of. JUDGE A.
